skill.name: human-docs-generator
skill.description: Generates concise, role-based human documentation for completed epics, breaking changes, architecture decisions, and deployed features.
skill.summary: Create short guides for developers, QA, DevOps, product teams, and end users.
skill.author: Brownbull

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported high risk because Markdown fences and examples were misread as shell execution, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance. Review found only documentation templates, Mermaid diagrams, and event examples in SKILL.md. No executable code, network access, prompt injection, or data exfiltration behavior was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Fence Backticks
low_findings.description: The external command detections are Markdown code fences for JSON, YAML, Mermaid, and Markdown examples. They do not execute shell commands or Ruby backtick expressions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is fenced documentation text, not executable code. No command string, shell invocation, or instruction to run those examples is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Detection
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography detections point to general documentation text and templates. No hash algorithm, cipher, crypto library call, or credential handling logic appears at these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines describe documentation goals, roles, and quality checks. I found no cryptographic implementation or unsafe algorithm selection.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Reconnaissance Detection
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance detections refer to Mermaid diagrams, placeholder template text, and user documentation examples. They do not perform network scans, host discovery, or system enumeration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is static Markdown guidance and diagram syntax. There is no evidence of probing, scanning, network access, or host information collection.

content.value_statement: Technical changes often leave each team with different questions and incomplete guidance. This skill turns project state into short, role-specific documents for faster onboarding and review.
actual_capabilities: Defines documentation outputs for frontend, backend, architecture, DevOps, QA, product, and end users.
Uses project state sources such as active work notes, knowledge files, logs, and test results.
Promotes concise documents under 1000 words with quickstarts, examples, diagrams, and screenshots.
Provides templates for frontend developer guides and end-user guides.
Defines input and output event shapes for documentation update workflows.
Lists quality checks and anti-patterns for keeping docs readable.
limitations: The skill is guidance only and does not include an executable generator script.
Output quality depends on the accuracy of available project notes and test results.
The 1000-word target can omit low-level implementation details.
Screenshots and diagrams still need verification against the current product.

use_cases.title: Create Developer Handoff Guides
use_cases.description: Generate concise frontend, backend, and architecture guides after an epic or major refactor is completed.

use_cases.title: Prepare QA and Release Notes
use_cases.description: Summarize current workflows, test focus areas, and recent feature behavior for validation before release.

use_cases.title: Explain Features to Business Users
use_cases.description: Turn technical implementation details into short end-user and product-manager guides with practical workflows.

prompt_templates.title: Generate Basic Role Docs
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for one completed feature.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the human-docs-generator skill to create short documentation for a completed feature. Include developer, QA, and end-user sections.

prompt_templates.title: Update Docs After an Epic
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for a larger completed workstream.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the completed epic notes, active state, and test results. Generate role-based human docs that reflect the current system only.

prompt_templates.title: Document a Breaking Change
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for migration and support guidance.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create updated human docs for a breaking change. Include affected roles, new workflows, diagrams, validation steps, and support notes.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Full Documentation Set
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for all supported audiences.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a complete human-docs set with an index, frontend guide, backend guide, architect guide, DevOps guide, tester guide, product guide, and end-user guide.

output_examples.input: A new password reset feature was shipped with frontend screens, backend endpoints, and QA results.
output: A frontend guide listing the changed pages, shared components, and state updates.
A backend guide describing the reset endpoint, token lifecycle, and service dependencies.
An end-user guide explaining how to request and complete a password reset.

output_examples.input: An API authentication flow changed and requires coordinated rollout.
output: An architecture guide with the current authentication flow diagram.
A DevOps guide with deployment and monitoring checks.
A tester guide with regression areas and expected behavior.

output_examples.input: A product dashboard was redesigned after several related tasks were completed.
output: A product-manager guide summarizing visible feature changes and metrics impact.
A QA guide covering chart, table, and export validation.
An end-user guide with simple steps for finding and using the dashboard.

best_practices: Provide current project state, test results, and recent change summaries before generation.
Keep each document focused on one role and under the recommended word limit.
Verify diagrams, screenshots, and workflow steps before sharing the docs.
anti_patterns: Do not use the output as a changelog or version history.
Do not mix all roles into one long document.
Do not publish generated instructions without checking them against the product.

faq.question: What does this skill generate?
faq.answer: It generates concise role-based documentation for developers, QA, DevOps, product managers, and end users.

faq.question: When should I use it?
faq.answer: Use it after an epic completes, a breaking change ships, an architecture decision changes, or a user feature is deployed.

faq.question: Does it run code or generate files automatically?
faq.answer: No. The skill provides documentation structure and generation instructions, but it does not include executable automation.

faq.question: Can it create diagrams?
faq.answer: Yes. It asks the assistant to use Mermaid diagrams for component trees, API flows, and user flows.

faq.question: How long should each document be?
faq.answer: The skill recommends keeping each role document under 1000 words.

faq.question: What inputs improve the results?
faq.answer: Provide active project notes, architecture decisions, operations logs, test results, and screenshots when available.
